title: Coryphantha vorwerkii
taxonomy: Coryphantha vorwerkii is a species of cactus in the family Cactaceae. This species is native to Mexico. Coryphantha vorwerkii is a type of pincushion cactus, characterized by its rounded stems and short, fine spines. It belongs to the genus Coryphantha, which is part of the tribe Cacteae. Coryphantha vorwerkii is also known as a rare species within its genus due to its limited geographical distribution.

intro: Coryphantha vorwerkii, also known as Vorwerk's pincushion cactus, is a species of small, globular cactus native to Mexico. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its purported health benefits, including its ability to lower blood pressure, reduce inflammation, and act as an antioxidant. The bioactive constituents of Coryphantha vorwerkii include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
